title: feat: route Claude Bedrock models through global inference profiles
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Switches all Claude models on Bedrock from US-regional (`us.`) to global (`global.`) cross-region inference profiles. Opus 4.8 was already on global; this brings the other four in line.

- Routes Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6 and Opus 4.7 through `global.anthropic.*` profiles
- Drops their cost blocks to the global base rates — the `us.` profile carries a 1.1× premium AWS charges for region-pinned routing: Haiku $1/$5, Sonnet $3/$15, Opus $5/$25 per M (in/out)
- Opus 4.7 was billing higher than 4.8 purely because of the regional premium; both are now $5/$25 and every model is ~10% cheaper
- Fixes the `aliases.test.ts` price-fallback test — its `flux` fixture broke when #11502 set flux to a 1.25× multiplier; the assertion is now multiplier-aware
- Regenerates the effective-prices snapshot (Opus 4.8 unchanged)

Verified: all `global.*` profiles ACTIVE and invokable with prod creds; local-gen e2e for all four models returns 200 with correct usage headers; Tinybird billing rows match the global rates exactly; worker tail clean of missing-conversion-rate warnings; 206/206 pricing tests pass.

gist.headline: Claude gets a passport
gist.blurb: We've switched our Claude models on AWS Bedrock to use global inference profiles. This means better availability and a sweet 10% price drop across the board for Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus.
gist.summary: Switched Claude models (Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Opus 4.7) on AWS Bedrock from US-regional to global cross-region inference profiles, removing the 1.1x regional premium.
gist.impact: Users will see a ~10% price drop across these Claude models, with Haiku now at $1/$5, Sonnet at $3/$15, and Opus at $5/$25 per million tokens.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee traveling or interacting with a globe, representing the switch to global inference profiles. A chunky robot companion (representing Claude/AI) holding a passport or a price tag with a discount.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny pilot's hat, spinning a glowing digital globe alongside a chunky pixel robot companion holding a small ticket. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
